questions.questionText: A compound '$$\mathrm{X}$$' when treated with phthalic anhydride in presence of concentrated $$\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{4}$$ yields '$$\mathrm{Y}$$'. '$$\mathrm{Y}$$' is used as an acid/base indicator. '$$\mathrm{X}$$' and '$$\mathrm{Y}$$' are respectively
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
Given below are two statements:

Statement I : Methyl orange is a weak acid.

Statement II : The benzenoid form of methyl orange is more intense/deeply coloured than the quinonoid form.

In the light of the above statement, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below:
options: (A) Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
(B) Statement $$\mathrm{I}$$ is incorrect but Statement II is correct
(C) Both statement I and Statement II are correct
(D) Statement I is correct but Statement II is incorrect
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: Following Kjeldahl's method, $1 \mathrm{~g}$ of organic compound released ammonia, that neutralised $10 \mathrm{~mL}$ of $2 \mathrm{M} ~\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$. The percentage of nitrogen in the compound is ___________ $\%$.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
Which of the following statements are correct?

A. Glycerol is purified by vacuum distillation because it decomposes at its normal boiling point.

B. Aniline can be purified by steam distillation as aniline is miscible in water.

C. Ethanol can be separated from ethanol water mixture by azeotropic distillation because it forms azeotrope.

D. An organic compound is pure, if mixed M.P. is remained same.

Choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below :
options:
(A) A, C, D only
(B) B, C, D only
(C) A, B, D only
(D) A, B, C only
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: An inorganic Compound 'X' on treatment with concentrated H<sub>2</sub>SO<sub>4</sub> produces brown fumes and gives dark brown ring with FeSO<sub>4</sub> in presence of concentrated H<sub>2</sub>SO<sub>4</sub>. Also Compound 'X' gives precipitate 'Y', when its solution in dilute HCl is treated with H<sub>2</sub>S gas. The precipitate 'Y' on treatment with concentrated HNO<sub>3</sub> followed by excess of NH<sub>4</sub>OH further gives deep blue coloured solution, Compound 'X' is :
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: The reagent neutral ferric chloride is used to detect the presence of ______________
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
An organic compound with 51.6% sulfur is heated in a Carius tube. The amount of this compound which will form 0.752 g of barium sulphate is ___________ $$\times$$ 10$$-$$1 g.

(Given molar mass of barium sulphate 233 g mol$$-$$1) (Nearest integer).
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
In Carius method of estimation of halogen, 0.25 g of an organic compound gave 0.15 g of silver bromide ( AgBr ). The percentage of Bromine in the organic compound is ________ $\times 10^{-1} \%$ (Nearest integer).

(Given : Molar mass of Ag is 108 and Br is $80 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$ )
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
$$0.400 \mathrm{~g}$$ of an organic compound $$(\mathrm{X})$$ gave $$0.376 \mathrm{~g}$$ of $$\mathrm{AgBr}$$ in Carius method for estimation of bromine. $$\%$$ of bromine in the compound $$(\mathrm{X})$$ is ___________.

(Given: Molar mass $$\mathrm{AgBr=188~g~mol^{-1}}$$

$$\mathrm{Br}=80 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$$)
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
A sample of $$0.125 \mathrm{~g}$$ of an organic compound when analyzed by Duma's method yields $$22.78 \mathrm{~mL}$$ of nitrogen gas collected over $$\mathrm{KOH}$$ solution at $$280 \mathrm{~K}$$ and $$759 \mathrm{~mm}\, \mathrm{Hg}$$. The percentage of nitrogen in the given organic compound is __________. (Nearest integer)

Given :

(a) The vapour pressure of water of $$280 \mathrm{~K}$$ is $$14.2 \mathrm{~mm} \,\mathrm{Hg}$$.

(b) $$\mathrm{R}=0.082 \mathrm{~L}$$ atm $$\mathrm{K}^{-1} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$$
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: Kjeldahl's method was used for the estimation of nitrogen in an organic compound. The ammonia evolved from 0.55 g of the compound neutralised 12.5 mL of 1 M H2SO4 solution. The percentage of nitrogen in the compound is _____________. (Nearest integer)
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: The technique used for purification of steam volatile water immiscible substances is :
options:
(A) Fractional distillation
(B) Distillation
(C) Fractional distillation under reduced pressure
(D) Steam distillation
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
Given below are two statements :

Statement I : Sulphanilic acid gives esterification test for carboxyl group.

Statement II : Sulphanilic acid gives red colour in Lassigne's test for extra element detection.

In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below :
options: (A) Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
(B) Statement I is correct but Statement II is incorrect
(C) Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
(D) Statement I is incorrect but Statement II is correct

In an estimation of bromine by Carius method, 1.6 g of an organic compound gave 1.88 g of AgBr. The mass percentage of bromine in the compound is _______.
(Atomic mass, Ag = 108, Br = 80 g mol–1)

questions.questionText:
Identify the incorrect statements regarding primary standard of titrimetric analysis.

(A) It should be purely available in dry form.

(B) It should not undergo chemical change in air.

(C) It should be hygroscopic and should react with another chemical instantaneously and stoichiometrically.

(D) It should be readily soluble in water.

(E) $$\mathrm{KMnO}_4$$ & $$\mathrm{NaOH}$$ can be used as primary standard.

Choose the correct answer from the options given below :

Statement I: In Lassaigne's test, the covalent organic molecules are transformed into ionic compounds.

Statement II: The sodium fusion extract of an organic compound having N and S gives prussian blue colour with $\mathrm{FeSO}_4$ and $\mathrm{Na}_4\left[\mathrm{Fe}(\mathrm{CN})_6\right]$

In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below
options:
(A) Both Statement I and Statement II are false
(B) Statement I is false but Statement II is true
(C) Both Statement I and Statement II are true
(D) Statement I is true but Statement II is false

The funnel was shaken vigorously and then kept aside. The ethyl acetate layer in the funnel contains :

questions.subject: Chemistry

Item-I (drug) Item-II (test)

Given below are two statements:

Statement I : Aqueous solution of K$$_2$$Cr$$_2$$O$$_7$$ is preferred as a primary standard in volumetric analysis over Na$$_2$$Cr$$_2$$O$$_7$$ aqueous solution.

Statement II : K$$_2$$Cr$$_2$$O$$_7$$ has a higher solubility in water than Na$$_2$$Cr$$_2$$O$$_7$$.

In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below:
options: (A) Statement I is true but Statement II is false
(B) Both Statement I and Statement II are false

questions.subject: Chemistry

The transformation occurring in Duma's method is given below :

$${C_2}{H_7}N + \left( {2x + {y \over 2}} \right)CuO \to xC{O_2} + {y \over 2}{H_2}O + {z \over 2}{N_2} + \left( {2x + {y \over 2}} \right)Cu$$

The value of y is ______________. (Integer answer)
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
Complete combustion of $X$ g of an organic compound gave $0.25$ g of $CO_2$ and $0.12$ g of $H_2O$. If the % of carbon is $25\%$ and of hydrogen is $4.89\%$, then $X = \underline{\phantom{xxx}} \times 10^{-3}$ g. (Nearest integer)

(Molar mass of C, H and O are 12, 1 and 16 g mol$^{-1}$ respectively.)

questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
Given below are two statements :

Statement I : In 'Lassaigne's Test', when both nitrogen and sulphur are present in an organic compound, sodium thiocyanate is formed.

Statement II : If both nitrogen and sulphur are present in an organic compound, then the excess of sodium used in sodium fusion will decompose the sodium thiocyanate formed to give NaCN and Na2S.

In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below :
options: (A) Both Statement I and Statement II are correct.
(B) Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect.
(C) Statement I is correct but Statement II is incorrect.
(D) Statement I is incorrect but Statement II is correct.

Statement I is true but Statement II is false

(Test)		 LIST II

questions.questionText: A toxic compound " A " when reacted with NaCN in aqueous acidic medium yields an edible cooking component and food preservative " B ". " B " is converted to " C " by diborane and can be used as an additive to petrol to reduce emission. "C" upon reaction with oleum at $140^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ yields an inhalable anesthetic " D ". Identify " A ", " B ", " C " & " D ", respectively :
options:
(A)  Methanol; acetic acid; ethanol; diethyl ether
(B)  Ethanol; acetonitrile; ethylamine; ethylene
(C)  Methanol; formaldehyde; methyl chloride; chloroform
(D) Acetaldehyde; 2-hydroxypropanoic acid; propanoic acid; dipropyl ether

D Benzoic acid + Cinnamic acid IV Ammoniacal silver nitrate

questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
Given below are two statements :

Statement (I) : 1,2,3-Trihydroxypropane can be separated from water by simple distillation.

Statement (II) : An azeotropic mixture cannot be separated by fractional distillation.

In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below :
options:
(C)

Statement I is true but Statement II is false

(D)

Statement I is false but Statement II is true
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: $\mathrm{R}_{\mathrm{f}}$ value for 2-methylpropene in a solvent system (Ethyl acetate + ether) is 0.42 . 2-methylpropene is treated with dilute $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$ to give major organic product $(\mathrm{X})$. $R_f$ value for $(X)$ in the same solvent system under identical condition will be:
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
Match List I with List II

List - I (Technique) List - II (Application
(A) Distillation (I) Separation of glycerol from spent-lye
(B) Fractional distillation (II) Aniline - Water mixture
(C) Steam distillation (III) Separation of crude oil fractions
(D) Distillation under reduced pressure (IV) Chloroform - Aniline

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
options:
(A) A-I, B-II, C-IV, D-III
(B) A-II, B-III, C-I, D-IV
(C) A-IV, B-I, C-II, D-III
(D) A-IV, B-III, C-II, D-I
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: In Carius method 0.2425 g of an organic compound gave 0.5253 g silver chloride. The percentage of chlorine in the organic compound is
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: Methylation of 10 g of benzene gave 9.2 g of toluene. Calculate the percentage yield of toluene __________. (Nearest integer)
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
In the sulphur estimation, 0.20 g of a pure organic compound gave 0.40 g of barium sulphate. The percentage of sulphur in the compound is __________ $\times 10^{-1} \%$.

(Molar mass : $\mathrm{O}=16, \mathrm{~S}=32, \mathrm{Ba}=137$ in $\mathrm{g} ~\mathrm{mol}^{-1}$ )
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:

List - I

 List - II

$$

questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: In Dumas' method for estimation of nitrogen, 0.5 gram of an organic compound gave 60 mL of nitrogen collected at 300 K temperature and 715 mm Hg pressure. The percentage composition of nitrogen in the compound (Aqueous tension at $300 \mathrm{~K}=15 \mathrm{~mm} \mathrm{Hg}$ ) is___________%.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: In Carius method, 0.75 g of an organic compound gave 1.2 g of barium sulphate, find percentage of sulphur (molar mass $32 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$ ). Molar mass of barium sulphate is $233 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
In Dumas method for estimation of nitrogen, 0.50 g of an organic compound gave 70 mL of nitrogen collected at 300 K and 715 mm pressure. The percentage of nitrogen in the organic compound is $\_\_\_\_$ $\%$.

(Aqueous tension at 300 K is 15 mm ).
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: A student has been given 0.314 g of an organic compound and asked to estimate Sulphur. During the experiment, the student has obtained 0.4813 g of barium sulphate. The percentage of sulphur present in the compound is _________. (Given Molar mass in g mol−1 S: 32, BaSO4: 233)
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: On a thin layer chromatographic plate, an organic compound moved by $$3.5 \mathrm{~cm}$$, while the solvent moved by $$5 \mathrm{~cm}$$. The retardation factor of the organic compound is ________ $$\times 10^{-1}$$.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
0.1 mol of the following given antiviral compound $(\mathrm{P})$ will weigh ________ $\times 10^{-1} \mathrm{~g}$ (nearest integer).

(Given : molar mass in $\mathrm{g} \mathrm{mol}^{-1} \mathrm{H}: 1, \mathrm{C}: 12, \mathrm{~N}: 14, \mathrm{O}: 16, \mathrm{~F}: 19, \mathrm{I}: 127$ )
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
The reaction of 4-methyloct-1-ene $(\mathbf{P}, 2.52 \mathrm{~g})$ with $\mathrm{HBr}$ in the presence of $\left(\mathrm{C}_6 \mathrm{H}_5 \mathrm{CO}\right)_2 \mathrm{O}_2$ gives two isomeric bromides in a $9: 1$ ratio, with a combined yield of $50 \%$. Of these, the entire amount of the primary alkyl bromide was reacted with an appropriate amount of diethylamine followed by treatment with aq. $\mathrm{K}_2 \mathrm{CO}_3$ to give a non-ionic product $\mathbf{S}$ in $100 \%$ yield.

The mass (in mg) of $\mathbf{S}$ obtained is ________.

[Use molar mass (in $\mathrm{g} \mathrm{mol}^{-1}$ ) : $\mathrm{H}=1, \mathrm{C}=12, \mathrm{~N}=14, \mathrm{Br}=80$ ]
questions.subject: Chemistry
